title: The Effects of Mindfulness Meditation on Nursing Students' Stress and Anxiety Levels
headline: The Effects of Mindfulness Meditation on Nursing Students' Stress and Anxiety Levels
summary: A randomized controlled trial of mindfulness / meditation examined stress & anxiety. The aim of this study was to evaluate the effects of a virtual mindfulness meditation intervention on stress and anxiety levels of 145 nursing students.
keyFindings: The aim of this study was to evaluate the effects of a virtual mindfulness meditation intervention on stress and anxiety levels of 145 nursing students.
Because of the twin demands of classroom and clinical workloads, nursing students experience greater amounts of stress and anxiety than the average college student.
limitations: Participants completed the Perceived Stress Scale and the Generalized Anxiety Disorder-7 Scale.
Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.

The practice studied (mindfulness / meditation) is not the Miracle of Mind practice, so its findings don't transfer directly — though the shared directed-attention component may make some transfer plausible (see “Why this matters for the 7-minute practice” below).
simplifiedTitle: The Effects of Mindfulness Meditation on Nursing Students' Stress and Anxiety Levels
aiSnippet: RCT, Nursing education perspectives (2024). The aim of this study was to evaluate the effects of a virtual mindfulness meditation intervention on stress and anxiety levels of 145 nursing students. Because of the twin demands of classroom and clinical workloads, nursing students experience greater amounts of stress and anxiety than the average college student.
whyItMatters: MoM shares directed attention (the focused-attention component) with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
